'''''UFC 303: Pereira vs. Procházka 2''''' was a mixed martial arts event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on June 29, 2024, at the [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas–Paradise, NV MSA|Las Vegas Metropolitan Area]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/02/ufc-303-306-date-location-fight-card-news|title=UFC 303, UFC 306 dates and locations announced|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-02-25|access-date=2024-02-25}}</ref>

==Background==
A welterweight bout between former [[UFC Featherweight Championship|UFC Featherweight]] and [[UFC Lightweight Championship|Lightweight Champion]] [[Conor McGregor]] and former three-time [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Lightweight World Championship|Bellator Lightweight World Champion]] (also a former UFC lightweight title challenger) [[Michael Chandler]] was expected to headline the event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/4/14/24126317/conor-mcgregor-vs-michael-chandler-finally-official-for-ufc-303|title=Conor McGregor vs. Michael Chandler finally official for UFC 303|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2024-04-14|access-date=2024-04-14}}</ref> The pairing previously coached on [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team McGregor vs. Team Chandler]] against each other.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/2/4/23585692/conor-mcgregor-michael-chandler-to-coach-tuf-31-clash-later-this-year|title=Conor McGregor, Michael Chandler to coach TUF 31, clash later this year|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Staff|date=2023-02-04|access-date=2024-04-14}}</ref> However on June 13, it was announced that McGregor was injured and the pairing would be postponed to a future date.<ref name="Changes">{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-303-conor-mcgregor-injured-vs-michael-chandler-alex-pereira-vs-jiri-prochazka-new-main-event|title=Conor McGregor injured; Alex Pereira vs. Jiri Prochazka set as new UFC 303 headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells|date=2024-06-13|access-date=2024-06-13}}</ref>

The new headliner for this event was a [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] rematch between current champion [[Alex Pereira]] (also former [[List of UFC champions#Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] and former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) and former champion [[Jiří Procházka]] (also the inaugural [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Light Heavyweight Championship|Rizin Light Heavyweight Champion]]).<ref name="Changes"/> They previously met at [[UFC 295]] in November 2023, where Pereira won the vacant title via a second-round TKO.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/10096935-alex-pereira-defeats-jiri-prochazka-by-ko-to-win-light-heavyweight-title-at-ufc-295|title=Alex Pereira defeats Jiří Procházka by KO to win light heavyweight title at UFC 295|publisher=bleacherreport.com|author=Alex Ballentine|date=2023-11-12|access-date=2024-02-18}}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between former champion [[Jamahal Hill]] and [[Khalil Rountree Jr.]] was expected to serve as the co-headliner for this event.<ref>{{cite news|author=Jay Anderson|title=Jamahal Hill books quick return, gets Khalil Rountree Jr. at UFC 303|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/04/19/jamahal-hill-books-quick-return-gets-khalil-rountree-jr-at-ufc-303/|publisher=cagesidepress.com|date=2024-04-19|access-date=2024-05-20}}</ref> However, Rountree withdrew from the event after unintentionally ingesting [[DHEA]] and contacting Senior Vice President of Anti-doping compliance Jeff Noviztky and UFC Vice President of Anti-doping compliance, Donna Marcolini to inform them of the matter.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Staff|date=2024-05-19|title=Khalil Rountree out of UFC 303 due to banned substance|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/05/18/khalil-rountree-out-of-ufc-303-due-to-banned-substance/|access-date=2024-05-19|publisher=cagesidepress.com}}</ref> Rountree was suspended for just two months as it was confirmed he received a tainted supplement.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-khalil-rountree-failed-drug-test-supplement-company-mistake-suspension|title=UFC: Khalil Rountree failed drug test due to supplement company's mistake, suspension reduced to 2 months|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-05-22|access-date=2024-05-22}}</ref> He was replaced by [[Carlos Ulberg]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-05-23|title=With Khalil Rountree out, Carlos Ulberg steps in to face Jamahal Hill at UFC 303|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-303-carlos-ulberg-vs-jamahal-hill-replace-khalil-rountree-drug-test-suspension-mma-fight-booking|access-date=2024-05-23|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun}}</ref> In turn, Hill pulled out due to injury and was replaced by former title challenger [[Anthony Smith (fighter)|Anthony Smith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-303-diego-lopes-vs-brian-ortega-added-refreshed-lineup-international-fight-week-conor-mcgregor|title=UFC 303: Diego Lopes vs. Brian Ortega added, Anthony Smith replaces Jamahal Hill vs. Carlos Ulberg|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-06-13|access-date=2024-06-13}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-303-news-jamahal-hill-reveals-list-of-injuries-behind-withdrawal-vows-return-soon|title=Jamahal Hill reveals list of injuries behind UFC 303 withdrawal, vows to 'be back soon'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2024-06-14|access-date=2024-06-15}}</ref> Subsequently, for unknown reasons, Ulberg pulled out and was replaced by [[Roman Dolidze]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/6/21/24183278/roman-dolidze-steps-in-on-8-days-notice-to-face-anthony-smith-at-ufc-303|title=Roman Dolidze steps in on 8 days' notice to face Anthony Smith at UFC 303|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2024-06-21|access-date=2024-06-22}}</ref>

A women's strawweight bout between former [[Invicta Fighting Championships#Atomweight Championship|Invicta FC Atomweight Champion]] [[Michelle Waterson-Gomez]] and [[Gillian Robertson]] was scheduled for [[UFC 302]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Jay Anderson|date=2024-03-16|title=Michelle Waterson-Gomez vs. Gillian Robertson booked for UFC 302 in Newark|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/03/16/michelle-waterson-gomez-vs-gillian-robertson-booked-for-ufc-302-in-newark/|access-date=2024-03-17|publisher=cagesidepress.com}}</ref> However, the bout was postponed to this event for unknown reasons.<ref name="Michelle vs. Gillian">{{Cite web|author=Julio Fernando Navas|date=2024-04-08|title=Michelle Waterson-Gomez vs. Gillian Robertson is postponed until UFC 303|url=https://www.mmaaldia.com/waterson-vs-robertson-agendada-para-el-ufc-303/|access-date=2024-04-09|publisher=mmaaldia.com}} {{in lang|es}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between [[Cody Durden]] and Carlos Hernandez was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|author=Vipin Raghuwanshi|date=2024-03-25|title=Cody Durden announces UFC 303 bout against Carlos Hernandez|url=https://www.itnwwe.com/mma/cody-durden-announces-ufc-303-bout-vs-carlos-hernandez/|access-date=2024-03-25|publisher=itnwwe.com}}</ref> However, Durden was pulled from the event for unknown reasons and replaced by [[Road to UFC Season 2]] flyweight winner Rei Tsuruya.<ref name="Durden out">{{Cite web|publisher=sherdog.com|title=Flyweight prospect Rei Tsuruya slated to face Carlos Hernandez at UFC 303|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Flyweight-Prospect-Rei-Tsuruya-Slated-to-Face-Carlos-Hernandez-at-UFC-303-193311|access-date=2024-04-12|author=David Andrew}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between former [[UFC Heavyweight Champion]] [[Andrei Arlovski]] and [[Martin Buday]] was expected to take place at [[UFC Fight Night 242]].<ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-04-08|title=Andrei Arlovski books 42nd UFC fight, faces Martin Buday in Las Vegas|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/04/ufc-news-andrei-arlovski-vs-martin-buday-las-vegas|access-date=2024-04-08|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref> However, the bout was moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-05-12|title=Former UFC champ Andrei Arlovski added to Conor McGregor vs. Michael Chandler card at UFC 303|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/05/ufc-303-fight-card-conor-mcgregor-vs-michael-chandler-adds-andrei-arlovski-martin-buday|access-date=2024-05-13|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn}}</ref>

A day before the event, the featherweight co-headliner bout between former [[UFC Featherweight Champion|UFC Featherweight Championship]] challenger [[Brian Ortega]] and [[Diego Lopes (fighter)|Diego Lopes]] was changed to a lightweight bout at the request of Ortega, reportedly due to weight-cutting issues. Lopes was already on weight, weighing at 146.5 when he was notified, and as such had to gain back up to 155 before the weigh ins.<ref>{{Cite web|author= Ken Hathaway|title=UFC 303 weigh-ins video: Brian Ortega, Diego Lopes hit marks for newly agreed-to lightweight co-main|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-303-weigh-ins-brian-ortega-diego-lopes-changed-lightweight-fight|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|date=2024-06-28|access-date=2024-06-28}}</ref> Subsequently, on the day of the event, Ortega withdrew from the bout due to an illness. He was replaced by [[Dan Ige]] just hours before the bout took place at a catchweight of 165 pounds.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-303-dan-ige-vs-diego-lopes-brian-ortega-withdraws|title=Dan Ige replaces Brian Ortega in shocking 11th-hour UFC 303 co-main event change vs. Diego Lopes|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-06-29|access-date=2024-06-29}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, [[Jean Silva]] weighed in at 147.5 pounds, one and a half pounds over the featherweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and Silva was fined 20 percent of his purse which went to his opponent [[Charles Jourdain]].<ref>{{Cite web|author=Alexander K.Lee|date=2024-06-28|title=UFC 303 weigh-in results: Alex Pereira vs. Jiri Prochazka 2 official, co-main has weight class changed|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/6/28/24188217/ufc-303-weigh-in-results-alex-pereira-vs-jiri-prochazka-2-official-co-main-has-weight-class-changed|access-date=2024-06-28|publisher=mmafighting.com}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bjpenn.com/mma-news/ufc-303/ufc-303-bonus-report-alex-pereira-one-of-six-fighters-to-take-home-50k/|title=UFC 303 Bonus Report: Alex Pereira one of six fighters to take home $50k|publisher=bjpenn.com|author=Chris Taylor|date=2024-06-29|access-date=2024-06-29}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Andre Fili vs. Cub Swanson'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Alex Pereira, Macy Chiasson, Joe Pyfer''', and '''Payton Talbott'''
